HANDANOVIC: "WE'RE DESERVEDLY IN THE CHAMPIONS LEAGUE"
The goalkeeper after making some vital stops against Empoli: "We had to suffer but winning was what mattered"

He was certainly a star tonight. Samir Handanovic’s extraordinary saves made the difference as Inter beat Empoli and the Slovenian looked back on the match to Inter TV: "Before the match, they presented me with the award for the best goalkeeper this season. I had to prove I deserved it…A goalkeeper is there to make saves. Let’s enjoy this moment now but we need to keep aiming higher."

"We were never afraid but we tried to manage the game and got it wrong. It happens, we had to suffer but we won in the end. It doesn’t matter how but we're in the Champions League deservedly. Compared to the final match last season, it was a different game. I want to thank the crowd, they were magnificent. It would have been tough without them, it almost felt like playing in a Champions League match. It’s true that the Champions League is different to the league but we needed that experience. Now, we can only depend on ourselves. We paid for that game against PSV but we’re celebrating today."

"Empoli are a tough side, they came to San Siro to win. The crossbar? D’Ambrosio saved us. I didn’t see him but I thanked him! I was told after that we were in the Europa League at moments tonight with results elsewhere but winning was the important thing and that’s what we did."

Inter secured Champions League football on the final day of the season, and begnning next year, the club will begin a new era under coach Antonio Conte. The former Chelsea coach is set to sign a contract this week with the nerazzurri, a three-year deal at a €12m net salary. On Wednesday the official announcement could be made, the same day that Conte's former club will take part in the Europa League final against Arsenal. The situation rests however on when the meeting between Inter and Spalletti to resolve the latter's contract.

Conte will be in Madrid the following Saturday for the Champions League final, and he should make his first official outing as the new coach of Inter together with the entire directorial staff (Marotta, Ausilio, Antonelli, and Zhang). In the coming weeks a visit to Chine for Conte is expected, to meet Zhang Jindong and Inter's ownership. The marriage between Conte and Inter is set to begin, the deal will be signed and the announcement will be made soon.

轉自新浪飛機文

近日，歐洲足協官方稱，國際米蘭已經成功達到了2015年簽下的FFP和解協議中的各項要求，將不再受到該協議的限制。

盈虧達標，國米成功退出FFP和解協議

財政公平法案（UEFA Financial Fair Play）是由歐洲足協在2010年出台的一項政策，旨在規範旗下賽事參賽球隊的財政行為，控制財政赤字限制不可持續投資方式，從而實現盈虧平衡的健康發展模式。

此前由於經營不佳等多方面原因，國際米蘭在2015年托希爾時期與歐洲足協簽署了FFP的和解協議，二者的協議條款主要包括財務指標如盈虧平衡要求、收購薪資、球隊陣容等方面。

伴隨蘇寧入主後的深耕細作，截至上季末，國米已基本達到和解協議的大部分約定，儘管歐聯報名人數仍受限，但已非常接近解除限制。終在18/19賽季行將結束之際，國際米蘭完全達成協議中要求的財務指標，成功退出FFP的和解協議。

管理行之有效，競技與營收實現雙贏

從2014-15賽季托希爾簽下和解協議，到今季完全達標，期間蘇寧的功勞不可磨滅，通過行之有效的管理，國際米蘭不僅時隔6年重返歐聯聯賽，還創造了球場上座人數、比賽日門票收入等方面的新紀錄，此外國際米蘭的組織管理架構也不斷得到完善優化，球會使用意大利本土管理層並採用聯席雙CEO的模式，此前的家族式管理也進化至現代化企業管理。

不斷提升的競技成績不僅贏得了更多球迷的關注，同時在電視轉播上也更受青睞，藍黑軍團主場上座人數連續兩個賽季突破100萬。此外，國際米蘭的營收狀況也在持續好轉，根據德勤年初發佈的足球球會財富排行榜，國際米蘭2017/18賽季收入達到2.81億歐元，相較於2014/2015賽季，國際米蘭收入已經提升1.16億歐元，相比此前一賽季增長7%，在所有足球球會中排名第14位，在意甲位列第二。

國米迎復興良機，未來發展或可期

表面看來，成功退出FFP和解協議帶來最直接的影響是球會能夠在歐戰中恢復到25人的報名名額，以及更加靈活的轉會策略，但觀其帶來的更深層次的影響，著實不可估量，尤其對於球會的復興會是一個轉折點。

上季由於和解協議的限制，國際米蘭先後錯失簡斯路和拉芬拿兩元大將，轉出的辛尼奧路又在羅馬大放異彩，今季兩場歐戰關鍵比賽也因報名人數較少導致遺憾出局，這些幾乎是國米在FFP和解協議限制下用人的一個縮影，而下季之後藍黑軍團基本可以避免這樣的情況發生。
接下來幾年，除了轉會市場操作遊刃有餘，歐戰賽場上也會有一筆可觀的獎金，藍黑軍團的未來發展大有可期。

今年6月，蘇寧就將入主國際米蘭三週年，過往賽季中，蘇寧可以說交出了一份令人信服的答卷，相信退出和解協議只是復興的開始，期待蘇寧攜手國米能讓外界看到更多的驚喜。

It's official: Antonio Conte will be Inter's new Coach!

👉 http://bit.ly/2EKRdNP #WelcomeAntonio #NotForEveryone

It's his gestures, the looks he gives, the details he goes into that define Antonio Conte. Ever since he was a child, his life has been dedicated to football. His passion and love for football meant it became an objective to pursue, and this resulted in him becoming a footballer. He sees football as the best way to express himself, to leave a mark, whether on the pitch or on the bench.

Determined and ambitious, Antonio Conte is now beginning his inter adventure. Right here before us, we have two entities with the same objective and outlook – that is to win. Head, heart and legs are the three words which sum up Conte’s career.

Head: He always had clear ideas when he played out on the pitch as a midfielder. His coaches were certain that he’d continue his career in the dugout.

Heart: This is something he has constantly shown on every adventure he’s been on: he’s always ensured that his teammates and players have felt the necessary sense of belonging to achieve the most important objectives.

Legs: Hard work is something he has always been known for. Training sessions and matches are to be experienced to the full.

Hard work and sweat, unity of purpose and identity. Something which Italy’s National Team displayed in the 2016 European Championship. Conte’s Italy, who were knocked out on penalties by Germany, thrilled and brought an entire nation together. He’s also left his mark abroad: he won the Premier League during his first season in England.

Now, Antonio Conte’s challenge is the same as the entire Nerazzurri world: to continue moving forward and to bring Inter back to the apex of European football.

"I’m sure that Antonio Conte is one of the best coaches out there," said Inter President Steven Zhang. "I’m certain that he’ll help us achieve our objective and fulfil our mission, one which has always remained the same: to make this club one of the best in the world again."

"A new chapter in my life is beginning, I’m really excited," commented Antonio Conte, Inter’s new Coach. "Through my work, I’ll try to repay all of the trust that the President and directors have placed in me. I have chosen Inter because of the Club it is, because of the project’s sound basis and how ambitious it is. Because of Inter’s history. I was struck by the Club’s transparency and the desire to bring Inter back to where it belongs."
#WelcomeAntonio!

It's his gestures, the looks he gives, the details he goes into that define Antonio Conte. Ever since he was a child, his life has been dedicated to football. His passion and love for football meant it became an objective to pursue, and this resulted in him becoming a footballer. He sees football as the best way to express himself, to leave a mark, whether on the pitch or on the bench.

Determined and ambitious, Antonio Conte is now beginning his inter adventure. Right here before us, we have two entities with the same objective and outlook – that is to win. Head, heart and legs are the three words which sum up Conte’s career.

Head: He always had clear ideas when he played out on the pitch as a midfielder. His coaches were certain that he’d continue his career in the dugout.

Heart: This is something he has constantly shown on every adventure he’s been on: he’s always ensured that his teammates and players have felt the necessary sense of belonging to achieve the most important objectives.

Legs: Hard work is something he has always been known for. Training sessions and matches are to be experienced to the full.

Hard work and sweat, unity of purpose and identity. Something which Italy’s National Team displayed in the 2016 European Championship. Conte’s Italy, who were knocked out on penalties by Germany, thrilled and brought an entire nation together. He’s also left his mark abroad: he won the Premier League during his first season in England.

Now, Antonio Conte’s challenge is the same as the entire Nerazzurri world: to continue moving forward and to bring Inter back to the apex of European football.

"I’m sure that Antonio Conte is one of the best coaches out there," said Inter President Steven Zhang. "I’m certain that he’ll help us achieve our objective and fulfil our mission, one which has always remained the same: to make this club one of the best in the world again."

"A new chapter in my life is beginning, I’m really excited," commented Antonio Conte, Inter’s new Coach. "Through my work, I’ll try to repay all of the trust that the President and directors have placed in me. I have chosen Inter because of the Club it is, because of the project’s sound basis and how ambitious it is. Because of Inter’s history. I was struck by the Club’s transparency and the desire to bring Inter back to where it belongs."

Today is Conte-day for Inter. The new nerazzurro manager is in Madrid with Inter management -- from Ausilio to Marotta: everyone is present. And in the upcoming hours there will be first contact with Zhang Jindong, who's arrived in the Spanish capital this morning. This will be the first meeting between Conte and the Inter owner.

The team will also begin thinking about the summer while in Madrid, specifically how to approach the transfer market. Inter is working to acquire Dzeko and Barella, two targets for some time now, and are also keeping an eye on Chiesa even though he will be challenging to obtain. Regarding Icardi, Inter would be available for a swap with Juventus for Dybala, but there are no conditions from an economic point of view, as the clubs have different evaluations of the two players' worth. It will be a long and intense summer for Inter, with Conte looking to give his new team shape.

replies.msg: 大家可以研究下，基艾沙同保列帶球能力都好，不過基艾沙比較難防守，佢比較多內切，所以會拉多一、二個守衞出來，比保列走邊更能拉散對方後防，這點似恩治斯奧；保列大家都知道佢能力及弱點，也就不談；如只看數據，有幾點會忽略的，基艾沙冇球走動很多（國米幾乎無人肯做），大局觀也不錯（保列最弱），最重要係佢鬥心超強（令我想起尼維特），我同意他價錢係貴，但佢可以幫到國米進攻套路，用佢搭LM 或基達做輔鋒會很有威力;但佢射術及至命傳送仍有很多改善空間

Despite being a complicated negotiation, the Icardi-Dybala swap deal between Inter and Juventus is still on the table, with the Nerazzurri's CEO Marotta keen on making it happen. No agreement between the two clubs has been reached yet, but the negotiations are everything but still, as the parties are trying to find a way to set up a deal.

In defence, the Nerazzurri are evaluating the opportunity to sign Aleksandar Kolarov from Roma. Marotta and sporting director Piero Ausilio, however, would pull the trigger on the deal only in case of very favourable conditions.

Finally, after being announced on Friday, Inter's new coach Antonio Conte will meet the club's owner Zhang Jindong on Saturday afternoon, in order to know him in person and discuss the objectives for the future.

quote.msg: 大家可以研究下，基艾沙同保列帶球能力都好，不過基艾沙比較難防守，佢比較多內切，所以會拉多一、二個守衞出來，比保列走邊更能拉散對方後防，這點似恩治斯奧；保列大家都知道佢能力及弱點，也就不談；如只看數據，有幾點會忽略的，基艾沙冇球走動很多（國米幾乎無人肯做），大局觀也不錯（保列最弱），最重要係佢鬥心超強（令我想起尼維特），我同意他價錢係貴，但佢可以幫到國米進攻套路，用佢搭LM 或基達做輔鋒會很有威力;但佢射術及至命傳送仍有很多改善空間

Inter Strike Deal With Roma Over Dzeko, Who Could Be Announced Tomorrow

Inter and Roma have reached a deal for Edin Dzeko worth €13.5 million according to a report from Sport Mediaset.The two clubs have been in talks over the Bosnian striker in recent weeks and have now finally bridged the gap between one another.Inter initially wanted to pay no more than €12 million whilst the Giallorossi were believed to be demanding a sum of €20 million.Inter and Dzeko already have a deal over personal terms and the 33-year-old will sign a two year deal worth €4 million a season to him.The report from the broadcaster goes on to report that the official announcement of Dzeko’s arrival at the Nerazzurri could come as early as tomorrow.His arrival would mark Inter’s first foray into the Summer transfer window and he certainly will not be the last through the door this Summer.During the 2018/19 season he made 40 appearances across all competitions in which he scored 14 goals and assisted 11 goals.

伊卡迪唔續約 又唔走 下季等坐夠1年

Inter Strike Deal With Roma Over Dzeko, Who Could Be Announced Tomorrow

Inter and Roma have reached a deal for Edin Dzeko worth €13.5 million according to a report from Sport Mediaset.The two clubs have been in talks over the Bosnian striker in recent weeks and have now finally bridged the gap between one another.Inter initially wanted to pay no more than €12 million whilst the Giallorossi were believed to be demanding a sum of €20 million.Inter and Dzeko already have a deal over personal terms and the 33-year-old will sign a two year deal worth €4 million a season to him.The report from the broadcaster goes on to report that the official announcement of Dzeko’s arrival at the Nerazzurri could come as early as tomorrow.His arrival would mark Inter’s first foray into the Summer transfer window and he certainly will not be the last through the door this Summer.During the 2018/19 season he made 40 appearances across all competitions in which he scored 14 goals and assisted 11 goals.

New Inter Coach Antonio Conte Cleans House – Icardi, Nainggolan & Perisic All For Sale
Newly appointed Inter coach Antonio Conte is close to implementing a revolution at his new club by showing Mauro Icardi, Ivan Perisic and Radja Nainggolan the door, Italian broadcaster Sky Sport Italia’s transfer market expert Gianluca Di Marzio reports.According to the report, there is no meeting scheduled between the Nerazzurri directors and Mauro Icardi’s agent Wanda Nara as has been reported. This is because the club’s position is clear concerning their former captain, he is no longer part of the club’s future plans.Icardi is only open to leaving Inter to join Juventus but he still decided that he does not want to leave Milano. Once he meets Antonio Conte during the pre-season training camp and is told that he is not part of the Italian coach’s plans, he will start to think about Juventus as a serious destination.Furthermore, Belgian midfielder Radja Nainggolan and Croatian international winger Ivan Perisic, are also among the players that could leave this summer.According to the report, Conte has decided to put both of them up for sale but only if a respectable offer is to arrive. However, Perisic is the only player whose future at inter Conte could reconsider and allow to stay.

Skriniar, Lautaro & Handanovic Are Conte’s Three Reference Points For New Inter Squad

Milan Skriniar, Lautaro Martinez and Samir Handanovic are the three players around whom Antonio Conte intends to build his new Inter squad for next season, according to Sky Sport.Skriniar recently signed a contract extension with the Nerazzurri through to 2023 and was never likely to leave, forming one of Europe’s most formidable central defensive departments alongside Stefan De Vrij and Diego Godin.

Lautaro scored nine goals in all competitions last season and is proving himself a dependable striker at a high level both with Inter and Argentina, although Mauro Icardi’s probable departure requires the club to find a more experienced head to partner him up front.

Handanovic arguably enjoyed his most impressive season at Inter during the 2018-19 camapign, taking over the captain’s armband when Icardi was stripped of it

.Icardi was then one of three players identified by Sky Sport as likely to leave the club this summer, along with Radja Nainggolan and Ivan Perisic.

Inter Sell Zinho Vanheusden & Xian Emmers To Standard Liege For €25M


Inter have sold Belgian duo Zinho Vanheusden and Xian Emmers to Belgian Division A side Standard Liege for a around €25 million according to Italian broadcaster Sky Sport Italia’s transfer market expert Gianluca Di Marzio.
The duo came up through the ranks at Inter’s youth team, the Primavera, with midfielder Xian Emmers, having spent this past season at Serie B side Cremonese. Central defender Zinho Vanheusden has been on loan at Standard Liege, the club he left to join Inter, since January 2018 where he has been playing regularly this past season, and has been doing so well, being voted Standard’s Man Of The Match on at least two occasions.
According to the report, this brings the Nerazzurri within €15 million of their €40 million goal of capital gains that the Nerazzurri have to produce by the end of June to finally be able to exit the terms set out in the Settlement Agreement with UEFA.

Central defender Zinho Vanheusden has been on loan at Standard Liege, the club he left to join Inter, since January 2018 where he has been playing regularly this past season, and has been doing so well, being voted Standard’s Man Of The Match on at least two occasions.
According to the report, this brings the Nerazzurri within €15 million of their €40 million goal of capital gains that the Nerazzurri have to produce by the end of June to finally be able to exit the terms set out in the Settlement Agreement with UEFA.

Nicolo Barella has his heart set on joining Inter despite Cagliari accepting an offer from Roma, reports Gianluca Di Marzio.

Barella is one of the most sought after midfielders in Serie A and has the likes of Roma and Inter battling for his signature.

Roma reached an agreement with Cagliari to sign the Italy international but the 22-year-old is holding out for Inter.

Roma are willing to use Luca Pellegrini and Gregoire Defrel as makeweights in a deal to sign the Cagliari midfielder, which their Serie A rivals are happy with.

However, Inter are Barella’s preferred destination even though Antonio Conte’s side are yet to reach an agreement for the midfielder.

Roma have put Alessandro Florenzi on the transfer market amid interest from Inter, according to Corriere dello Sport.Florenzi was due to replace Daniele De Rossi as the Giallorossi’s captain but could be sacrificed because of his frosty relationship with the club’s supporters.Antonio Conte wanted to sign the versatile midfielder while managing Juventus and has asked Beppe Marotta and Piero Ausilio to tempt him with an offer.Inter would be willing to meet Roma’s €25 million asking price for Florenzi, while offering €5-10 million more in add-ons, although they would prefer a part-exchange deal involving Dalbert and €5-10 million in cash.The Nerazzurri would be even happier to include the Italian in a swap deal with Edin Dzeko and Mauro Icardi, although the latter’s desire to remain in Milan makes this highly improbable.Icardi would not accept a move to a club which does not play in the Champions League, while Roma are reluctant to take on another player on a high salary as they seek to downscale their wage bill.Florenzi remains ‘a concrete possibility’ for Inter, though, and could represent the ideal player to fill in at wing-back for Conte.

Inter wants Stefano Sensi and therefore, have tried to make a hard push to sign the 1995 born Italian midfielder. Today, the Nerazzurri club met Sassuolo's director (Giovanni Carnevali) and the player's agent (Giuseppe Riso) to discuss the potential transfer. Riso, who was Intercepted by SkySport's microphones at the exit of the Inter headquarters, stated: "It was a meeting to see if this transfer could happen. We are thinking about it. Many teams like Sensi; now we've talked to Inter. There are good feelings".

Fabrizio Romano

Inter’s chief executive Alessandro Antonello today confirmed the Nerazzurri would work together with Milan to build a new stadium.

Both clubs want to knock down San Siro and replace it with a more modern stadium on the same ground, with a plan to be submitted to Milan city council soon.

Antonello joined Milan president Paolo Scaroni in Lausanne earlier to support Milan and Cortino d’Ampezzo’s joint bid to host the 2026 Winter Olympics.

“We’re going to build a new San Siro next to where the old one was in the same concession area,” Scaroni told reporters.

“The old one is going to be knocked down and building work will start to create another ground.”

La Repubblica last week reported Inter and Milan’s new stadium would cost approximately €700 million and could be ready for the start of the 2022-23 season.

Neither club was interested in renovating the current stadium because it would have taken longer and forced them to play their home fixtures at a neutral venue towards the end of building work.

Inter’s Deal For Sensi Delayed As Youngster Vergani Hasn’t Accepted Sassuolo Yet

Inter’s deal for midfielder Stefano Sensi could be delayed because their academy forward Edoardo Vergani has not accepted a move to Sassuolo yet, according to FcInterNews.it. Sensi will join Inter on a season-long loan worth €5 million with an option to buy for €20 million, but Vergani – who is valued at €5 million – will cover the cost of the initial loan.The 18-year-old has not yet given the green light to join the Neroverdi, though, because he also has an offer from Fiorentina. Sky Sport contradicted this report later on Tuesday night, though, saying Vergani would join Roma as part of a deal to bring Edin Dzeko to Inter.Inter and Sassuolo are not concerned by the hold-up in any case and would be able to find another youngster to include in the deal should Vergani go to the Giallorossi. The forward made 17 appearances for Inter’s Primavera side last season, scoring twice in the annual Viareggio tournament. Sensi has agreed to sign for Inter and should become the Nerazzurri’s first acquisition of the summer whenever the 23-year-old’s arrival is officially announced.

However, Inter will have a possible fast lane in the future for the player - they are very interested in the youngster but would like to see him grow.

Inter’s Deal For Sensi Delayed As Youngster Vergani Hasn’t Accepted Sassuolo Yet

Inter’s deal for midfielder Stefano Sensi could be delayed because their academy forward Edoardo Vergani has not accepted a move to Sassuolo yet, according to FcInterNews.it. Sensi will join Inter on a season-long loan worth €5 million with an option to buy for €20 million, but Vergani – who is valued at €5 million – will cover the cost of the initial loan.The 18-year-old has not yet given the green light to join the Neroverdi, though, because he also has an offer from Fiorentina. Sky Sport contradicted this report later on Tuesday night, though, saying Vergani would join Roma as part of a deal to bring Edin Dzeko to Inter.Inter and Sassuolo are not concerned by the hold-up in any case and would be able to find another youngster to include in the deal should Vergani go to the Giallorossi. The forward made 17 appearances for Inter’s Primavera side last season, scoring twice in the annual Viareggio tournament. Sensi has agreed to sign for Inter and should become the Nerazzurri’s first acquisition of the summer whenever the 23-year-old’s arrival is officially announced.

Tommaso Giulini, president of Cagliari, made the point about Barella's future: "It seems disrespectful for Cagliari to talk about the auction - he declared at the exit of the League A League offices - With Inter we have an agreement on the fixed part since June 11th, which I think is the most important thing in any negotiation, and there were only the bonuses to be discussed, but it's been 20 days since they disappeared, I don't know if I'm more interested in the boy.

In the meantime, an even more important proposal from Rome has arrived and we have closed with them, we are satisfied. Now I think it's legitimate for the boy to take two days to decide his future. He has never been oriented towards anything and has never had any agreements with anyone, he is taking a few days to evaluate the new situation. We hope that the issue will close soon ".

Sky - This is Inter's final offer for Nicolo Barella: €36m plus €4m in bonuses
Inter want to close for Nicolo Barella and want to do it on their own terms. Ausilio and Marotta do not intend to raise the offer presented a few weeks ago despite the strong inclusion of Roma. The player has already chosen Inter for some time, he wants to play for Antonio Conte and has already said no multiple times to the Giallorossi.

According to Sky Sport transfer expert Fabrizio Romano, the offer remains on the table is €36m plus €4m in bonuses. No player(s) included. This is the final offer of Inter and they will reiterate it again in the face-to-face meeting with Cagliari in the next few days.

Roma’s new head coach Paulo Fonseca today told reporters Edin Dzeko cannot stay at the club if he is not motivated.Inter have been in talks with the Giallorossi to sign the Bosnian for several weeks, but the clubs are nowhere near an agreement at present.Fonseca said he had not spoken to the 33-year-old yet but assured he did not want Dzeko if his head was not in the right place.“I only want players who are happy to be here and ready to fight for this club,” Fonseca said.“I want players who are dedicated to the cause and determined to pursue our objectives. This is a prerequisite for me.”Dzeko agreed personal terms with the Nerazzurri last month but was forced to turn up for Roma’s preseason training today.Antonio Conte had hoped to sign Dzeko and Romelu Lukaku before this week but he will be forced to work with Mauro Icardi for now.

Roma’s new head coach Paulo Fonseca today told reporters Edin Dzeko cannot stay at the club if he is not motivated.
Inter have been in talks with the Giallorossi to sign the Bosnian for several weeks, but the clubs are nowhere near an agreement at present.
Fonseca said he had not spoken to the 33-year-old yet but assured he did not want Dzeko if his head was not in the right place.
“I only want players who are happy to be here and ready to fight for this club,” Fonseca said.“
I want players who are dedicated to the cause and determined to pursue our objectives. This is a prerequisite for me.”
Dzeko agreed personal terms with the Nerazzurri last month but was forced to turn up for Roma’s preseason training today.
Antonio Conte had hoped to sign Dzeko and Romelu Lukaku before this week but he will be forced to work with Mauro Icardi for now.

Roma’s new head coach Paulo Fonseca today told reporters Edin Dzeko cannot stay at the club if he is not motivated.
Inter have been in talks with the Giallorossi to sign the Bosnian for several weeks, but the clubs are nowhere near an agreement at present.
Fonseca said he had not spoken to the 33-year-old yet but assured he did not want Dzeko if his head was not in the right place.
“I only want players who are happy to be here and ready to fight for this club,” Fonseca said.“
I want players who are dedicated to the cause and determined to pursue our objectives. This is a prerequisite for me.”
Dzeko agreed personal terms with the Nerazzurri last month but was forced to turn up for Roma’s preseason training today.
Antonio Conte had hoped to sign Dzeko and Romelu Lukaku before this week but he will be forced to work with Mauro Icardi for now.

Nicolò Barella Could Have Inter Medical On Friday

Nicolò Barella could undergo a medical with Inter as early as Friday, according to FcInterNews.it. The midfielder is on the verge of completing his move to the Nerazzurri after they agreed a deal with Cagliari in a meeting yesterday. Inter met Barella’s agent Alessandro Beltrami again today and informed him of the good news, telling Beltrami they will meet the Rossoblu on Wednesday to finalise the agreement.
Barella will sign a five-year deal and earn €2.5 million per season, having agreed personal terms several weeks ago.The 22-year-old is on holiday at the moment after representing Italy in the European Under-21 Championships last month.
However, Barella will interrupt his vacation to complete the medical and sign his new contract if Inter and Cagliari can sort everything before Friday. Antonio Conte wanted at least one more reinforcement in midfield before starting the new season, and it seems Inter are close to satisfying his request.

THE NEW MILANO STADIUM
Inter and AC Milan today filed the “Technical and Economic Feasibility Study” for the new Milan stadium and its multifunctional district

Today, F.C. Internazionale Milano S.p.A. and A.C. Milan S.p.A. filed with the Municipality of Milan the “Technical and Economic Feasibility Study” for the new Milan stadium and its multifunctional district.

The proposal to the local institutions marks a first official step by the Clubs, beginning a shared journey together with the Municipality towards constructing a modern, sustainable and accessible urban district in the San Siro area, built around a new world-class stadium. The documentation submitted by the Clubs consists of a detailed technical and engineering study which does not yet include an architectural component.

The project aims at enhancing the San Siro area with the realization of:

• a new modern stadium, of approx. 60.000 seats, in the area adjacent to the existing Meazza (owned by the City of Milan and currently given in concession to the two Clubs);

• a multifunctional district in the area where the Meazza currently stands, dedicated to sports, entertainment, and shopping, that will become a vibrant destination for citizens, fans and tourists 365-days/year, creating jobs for over 3,500 people.

This is a comprehensive project that foresees private investments of over 1.2 billion euros and the implementation of which is essential in bringing Milan’s football back to the elite of the European and global football arena. The Feasibility Study has been prepared in accordance with the so-called Stadium Law, which promotes the development of sports infrastructures in Italy. All investments will be carried out by FC Internazionale Milano and AC Milan, who in exchange will receive a 90-years’ surface right. This right is expected to be assigned through a public tender for which the Clubs, as promoters, will retain a right to match.

The request made today by FC Internazionale Milano and AC Milan to the public administration aims at obtaining the declaration of public interest from the Municipality of Milan. Only thereafter, the Clubs will proceed with the filing of a definitive plan, which will include all architectural components. In this regard, a competitive process will be launched to select architects among the best international firms specializing in the design of sporting infrastructure. The Clubs also aim at establishing an open and constructive dialogue with the local community, with the idea of optimizing the project by taking into consideration their needs and aspirations for the San Siro area.

The choice of FC Internazionale Milano and AC Milan is based on a careful and in-depth analysis, carried out over the last few months, of the different available options (including the restructuring of the current Meazza stadium). It is inspired by the best benchmarks of international stadia and entertainment districts (almost all made ex-novo) around the world, as well as by Italian culture and the unique character of the City of Milan. This analysis shows that the construction of a new building is from all perspectives the best possible solution both for the Clubs and for the city of Milan: it guarantees the best user experience, sustainability, adequate security, safety and accessibility standards, and honours Milan’s reputation for innovation and internationalization.

The Feasibility Project explains in detail the reasons why the construction of the new stadium is preferred to the renovation of the Meazza: the current structure presents a series of technical issues, of which the solution would have required a complete refurbishment, with enormous execution risks, possible cost overruns and unpredictable complications. In addition, there would have been organizational issues linked to the simultaneous management of football matches, longer construction timings, revenue loss connected to the temporary reduction of the available capacity to even less than 45,000 seats, safety concerns and the need to hold home matches outside the city of Milan.

Both FC Internazionale Milano and AC Milan are firmly convinced that this is the right path to follow and are thrilled and excited by the project which will give Milan a new landmark of world class excellence, contributing to the progress already made by the City of Milan over the last few years.

Inter Complete Loan With Obligation Deal For Cagliari Midfielder Barella

Inter have reached a full agreement with Cagliari to sign Nicolò Barella, according to Sky Sport.
The midfielder will join on a loan deal worth €10 million with an obligation to buy for €30 million, but the final fee could rise as high as €50 million. Inter will also include €10 million in add-ons for Barella, but €5 million will be easy to activate while the other €5 million are harder to trigger. It is therefore likely that the total fee Cagliari will receive is €45 million, slightly short of their original €50 million asking price.
Barella is on holiday at the moment after representing Italy at this summer’s European Under-21 Championships, but he is ready to return for a medical at any moment. Antonio Conte might be able to include the 22-year-old in his squad for Inter’s preseason tour of Asia, for which they will fly out on 18 July.
Barella has been adamant for weeks that Inter were the only club he wanted to move to, and it looks as if his dream is about to become reality.

有消息稱，國米已經向曼聯提出了7500萬歐元分兩年支付的報價，但遭到曼聯方面的拒絕。一方面的原因是曼聯對盧卡古的估價在7500萬英鎊而不是歐元。

另一方面的原因是，當年曼聯以4500萬歐元報價比列錫時，國米咬死5000萬歐元不鬆口，因此曼聯現在的做法一定程度上有種「報復」的意味。
